figures.caption: Table 1: Summary of best proposed algorithm per metric and VLA (↑ = higher is better), with gains over both baselines.

Figure 1: Cumulative failures discovered during 200 testing iterations across four VLA models. Each curve represents one testing method, while shaded regions show the interquartile range over 10 runs. The proposed methods consistently discover failures faster than both baselines on EO1, GR00T-N1.6, and π0, whereas all methods converge on OpenVLA7b due to a ceiling effect.

Figure 5: Failed Object Coverage (%) distributions. Weighted_RF consistently achieves the highest FOC while simultaneously delivering the largest FR gains, demonstrating that RF-guided weighted scoring avoids object-concentration bias.

Figure 4: Trajectory Coverage under Failure, TCF (%) distributions. RF-based variants (Sorting_RF, Weighted_RF) exceed both baselines on most VLAs, indicating that ensemble-guided generation discovers behaviourally diverse failures. The DT-only variant (FSCSART_DT) occasionally falls below B2 due to over-exploitation of a single failure cluster.

Figure 3: Trajectory Coverage (%) distributions. Proposed methods are broadly competitive with or exceed both baselines, confirming that ML-guided failure exploitation does not collapse behavioural exploration.

Figure 2: Final Failure Rate (%) distributions for all algorithms across four VLA models. Annotated values are medians. Proposed methods substantially exceed both B1: Random and B2: FSCSART on EO1, GR00T-N1.6, and π0.
